slices.title: Workflow state bootstrap
slices.goal: Introduce canonical slice tracking artifacts for roadmap-driven work.
slices.summary: Create machine-readable slice state and a human-readable roadmap before the first product slice.
acceptance: Current and completed slices are explicit.
slices.notes: Bootstrapped as part of repository initialization.

slices.title: Python CLI scaffold baseline
slices.goal: Create the minimal installable Python CLI scaffold with config init and validation.
slices.summary: Add pyproject, src layout, typed config domain models, JSON persistence, and CLI commands.
acceptance: fqdn-updater init writes a valid scaffold config.
fqdn-updater config validate accepts the scaffold and rejects invalid files.
Ruff and pytest can be run once dependencies are installed.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Config domain model expansion
slices.goal: Deepen typed config models and validation rules toward router and mapping UX.
slices.summary: Extend config schema with stricter contracts while staying local-only.
acceptance: Router, service, and mapping config rules are locally validated.
Validation errors are actionable and deterministic.
slices.notes: Completed with stronger slug, list uniqueness, source URL presence, and allowed_source_ips validation on 2026-04-08.

slices.title: Source registry scaffold
slices.goal: Introduce explicit service source registry contracts without fetching data yet.
slices.summary: Add typed source definitions and initial registry wiring.
acceptance: Registry access stays out of CLI formatting code.

slices.title: Logging and run artifact scaffold
slices.goal: Prepare machine-readable run artifacts and logging boundaries.
slices.summary: Add typed run result skeletons and artifact persistence abstractions.

slices.title: RCI client contracts
slices.goal: Define transport-facing interfaces and RCI-only client contracts.
slices.summary: Add infrastructure interfaces without implementing sync behavior yet.
acceptance: No HTTP details leak into domain or application.

slices.title: Workflow enforcement gates
slices.goal: Add enforced verification and repo-local workflow rules for slice execution.
slices.summary: Introduce a canonical verify wrapper, CI gate, and explicit edit autonomy and sandbox rules.
acceptance: A single verify wrapper exists and runs the required checks.
CI executes the verify wrapper on push and pull request.
Repository instructions explicitly allow non-destructive in-workspace edits without extra confirmation.
Repository instructions explicitly state that sandbox is still expected.
slices.notes: Completed with green local verification on 2026-04-08.
